title: New York State Route 253

infobox.value: [NY 383](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_383 "New York State Route 383") in [Scottsville](/wiki/Scottsville,_New_York "Scottsville, New York")

[I-90 Toll](/wiki/Interstate_90_(New_York) "Interstate 90 (New York)") / [New York Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") / [I-390](/wiki/Interstate_390_(New_York) "Interstate 390 (New York)") in [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York")
[NY 65](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_65 "New York State Route 65") in [Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(town),_New_York "Pittsford (town), New York")

* [Interstate](/wiki/List_of_Interstate_Highways_in_New_York "List of Interstate Highways in New York") * [US](/wiki/List_of_U.S._Routes_in_New_York "List of U.S. Routes in New York") * [State](/wiki/List_of_state_routes_in_New_York "List of state routes in New York") * [Reference](/wiki/List_of_reference_routes_in_New_York "List of reference routes in New York") * [Parkways](/wiki/Parkways_in_New_York "Parkways in New York") | |

(**NY 253**) is a 10.83-mile-long (17.43 km) east–west [state highway](/wiki/State_highway "State highway") in [Monroe County, New York](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York"), in the United States. The western terminus of the route is at [NY 383](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_383 "New York State Route 383") in the village of [Scottsville](/wiki/Scottsville,_New_York "Scottsville, New York"). Its eastern terminus is at [NY 65](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_65 "New York State Route 65") in the [town of Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(town),_New_York "Pittsford (town), New York"). NY 253 meets [Interstate 390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") (I-390) and connects to the [New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") ([I-90](/wiki/Interstate_90_in_New_York "Interstate 90 in New York")) in the town of [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York"). The majority of NY 253 passes through either rural or residential areas; however, the midsection of NY 253 is located in a heavily commercial portion of Henrietta.

State highway in Monroe County, New York, US

**New York State Route 253** (**NY 253**) is a 10.83-mile-long (17.43 km) east–west [state highway](/wiki/State_highway "State highway") in [Monroe County, New York](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York"), in the United States. The western terminus of the route is at [NY 383](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_383 "New York State Route 383") in the village of [Scottsville](/wiki/Scottsville,_New_York "Scottsville, New York"). Its eastern terminus is at [NY 65](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_65 "New York State Route 65") in the [town of Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(town),_New_York "Pittsford (town), New York"). NY 253 meets [Interstate 390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") (I-390) and connects to the [New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") ([I-90](/wiki/Interstate_90_in_New_York "Interstate 90 in New York")) in the town of [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York"). The majority of NY 253 passes through either rural or residential areas; however, the midsection of NY 253 is located in a heavily commercial portion of Henrietta.

NY 253 originally extended from [NY 36](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_36 "New York State Route 36") in [Mumford](/wiki/Mumford,_New_York "Mumford, New York") to [NY 64](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_64 "New York State Route 64") south of the [village of Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(village),_New_York "Pittsford (village), New York") when it was assigned as part of the [1930 renumbering of state highways in New York](/wiki/1930_state_highway_renumbering_(New_York) "1930 state highway renumbering (New York)"). By 1938, it was truncated to Scottsville on its western end but also extended northeastward to [Penfield](/wiki/Penfield,_New_York "Penfield, New York"). The portion of NY 253 through [East Rochester](/wiki/East_Rochester,_New_York "East Rochester, New York") and Penfield was realigned twice in the 1960s and 1970s before the route was cut back to NY 65 in Pittsford in the 1980s. Since that time, NY 253 has also been rerouted twice in Henrietta.

## Route description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_253&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Route description")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/NY_253_westbound_from_I-390.jpg/250px-NY_253_westbound_from_I-390.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_253_westbound_from_I-390.jpg)

NY 253 westbound as seen from the [I-390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") southbound overpass in Henrietta

NY 253 begins at an intersection with [NY 383](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_383 "New York State Route 383") in the village [Scottsville](/wiki/Scottsville,_New_York "Scottsville, New York"). It heads east out of the village and into the surrounding town of [Wheatland](/wiki/Wheatland,_New_York "Wheatland, New York") as Scottsville–West Henrietta Road. Just after crossing the [Genesee River](/wiki/Genesee_River "Genesee River") into the town of [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York"), NY 253 turns northward to briefly parallel the river. After about 0.25 miles (0.40 km), Scottsville–West Henrietta Road turns eastward; however, NY 253 continues northward onto Erie Station Road. The roadway gradually curves to the east, coming within 50 yards (46 m) of the [New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") ([I-90](/wiki/Interstate_90_in_New_York "Interstate 90 in New York")) and serving the highway's Scottsville [service area](/wiki/Rest_area "Rest area"). NY 253 and the Thruway follow parallel routings for 0.5 miles (0.8 km) before NY 253 shifts slightly south to intersect East River Road, a highway connecting the [village of Avon](/wiki/Avon_(village),_New_York "Avon (village), New York") to the city of [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York").[[3]](#cite_note-bing-3)

East of River Road, NY 253 [crosses](/wiki/Grade_crossing "Grade crossing") the [Livonia, Avon and Lakeville Railroad](/wiki/Livonia,_Avon_and_Lakeville_Railroad "Livonia, Avon and Lakeville Railroad") (LAL) and continues to parallel the Thruway through a lightly populated area of Henrietta toward the [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") of West Henrietta. Instead of entering the hamlet, NY 253 leaves Erie Station Road and follows an industrial parkway named Thruway Park Drive around the northwestern edge of the community. Upon reaching the eastern end of Thruway Park Drive at [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15"), NY 253 turns north, joining NY 15 on West Henrietta Road and crossing over the Thruway.[[3]](#cite_note-bing-3)

NY 15 and NY 253 continue northward along the heavily-developed and entirely commercial West Henrietta Road to Lehigh Station Road, where NY 253 splits from NY 15 and follows Lehigh Station Road eastward. Like West Henrietta Road before it, Lehigh Station Road is a major commercial strip, consisting of hotels, restaurants, and other establishments. This trend begins to cease as NY 253 meets [I-390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") (and indirectly with the Thruway) at an interchange, and ceases entirely at a junction with Middle Road, a largely alternate route to I-390 between [NY 251](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_251 "New York State Route 251") in Rush and Calkins Road.[[3]](#cite_note-bing-3)
Past Middle Road, NY 253 enters a predominantly residential neighborhood. It crosses a spur of the LAL that serves a pair of businesses on NY 253 and continues to the official town center of Henrietta, where it intersects [NY 15A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15A "New York State Route 15A"). NY 253 continues onward, meeting Pinnacle Road, a highway leading from NY 251 to [NY 252](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_252 "New York State Route 252"), 1 mile (1.6 km) east of NY 15A. East of Pinnacle Road, the amount of development along NY 253 declines sharply, and it continues eastward through open fields to its eastern terminus at [NY 65](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_65 "New York State Route 65") about 1 mile (1.6 km) east of the Henrietta–[Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(town),_New_York "Pittsford (town), New York") town line.[[3]](#cite_note-bing-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_253&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

### Establishment and early routing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_253&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Establishment and early routing")]

NY 253 was assigned as part of the [1930 renumbering of state highways in New York](/wiki/1930_state_highway_renumbering_(New_York) "1930 state highway renumbering (New York)"). It initially began at [NY 36](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_36 "New York State Route 36") in the [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") of [Mumford](/wiki/Mumford,_New_York "Mumford, New York") and followed Scottsville–Mumford Road to the village of [Scottsville](/wiki/Scottsville,_New_York "Scottsville, New York"). NY 253 had [overlaps](/wiki/Overlap_(road) "Overlap (road)") with [NY 251](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_251 "New York State Route 251") and [NY 35](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_35_(1927_%E2%80%93_early_1940s) "New York State Route 35 (1927 – early 1940s)") through Scottsville, then continued east on Scottsville–West Henrietta, East River, and Erie Station roads to the [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York") hamlet of West Henrietta. Here, it turned north to follow [NY 2](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_2_(1927%E2%80%931938) "New York State Route 2 (1927–1938)") (now [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15")) to Lehigh Station Road, which it followed eastward to its end at [NY 65](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_65 "New York State Route 65") in the [town of Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(town),_New_York "Pittsford (town), New York"). NY 253 continued on, however, joining NY 65 northward to Calkins Road. It then followed Calkins and Mendon Center Roads to [NY 64](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_64 "New York State Route 64") south of the [village of Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(village),_New_York "Pittsford (village), New York").[[2]](#cite_note-1930nyt-2)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/NY_253_fmr_nt.jpg/250px-NY_253_fmr_nt.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_253_fmr_nt.jpg)

The junction of [NY 441](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_441 "New York State Route 441") and Linear Park Drive was the eastern terminus of NY 253 from the late 1930s to the 1960s.

NY 253 was extended northward through Pittsford and [East Rochester](/wiki/East_Rochester,_New_York "East Rochester, New York") by 1936 along NY 64, [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15_(1924%E2%80%931938) "New York State Route 15 (1924–1938)") (now [NY 96](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_96 "New York State Route 96")), and Washington Street to a new terminus at [NY 33](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_33 "New York State Route 33") (modern [NY 441](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_441 "New York State Route 441")) in [Penfield](/wiki/Penfield,_New_York "Penfield, New York").[[5]](#cite_note-5) NY 35 was rerouted south of Scottsville c. 1938 to follow the routing of NY 253 to Mumford. As a result, NY 253 was truncated to the northern terminus of its overlap with NY 35 (modern [NY 383](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_383 "New York State Route 383")) north of the village center.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### Penfield realignments and truncation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_253&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Penfield realignments and truncation")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/22/NY_253_ref_marker.JPG/250px-NY_253_ref_marker.JPG)](/wiki/File:NY_253_ref_marker.JPG)

[Reference marker](/wiki/Reference_marker_(New_York) "Reference marker (New York)") for NY 253 on Five Mile Line Road, a remnant of its former routing in [Penfield](/wiki/Penfield,_New_York "Penfield, New York")

The bridge carrying NY 253 over [Irondequoit Creek](/wiki/Irondequoit_Creek "Irondequoit Creek") in Penfield's Linear Park was closed to vehicular traffic in the 1960s.[[8]](#cite_note-gn-8) The portion of North Washington Street north of the creek, now severed from the rest of the route, was renamed Linear Park Drive.[[9]](#cite_note-9) NY 253, meanwhile, was rerouted north of the junction of North Washington Street and Linden Avenue to follow Linden Avenue eastward across Irondequoit Creek to Whitney Road in [Perinton](/wiki/Perinton,_New_York "Perinton, New York"). It then turned north to follow Whitney and Five Mile Line Roads to the Four Corners of Penfield, where it ended at NY 441.[[10]](#cite_note-1968map-10)

An extension of Panorama Trail, named Panorama Trail South, was built from Panorama Creek Drive south to North Washington Street in the late 1960s and early 1970s to connect Washington to the new NY 441 [limited-access highway](/wiki/Limited-access_highway "Limited-access highway") being constructed through the towns of [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York") and Penfield. NY 253 was rerouted by 1971 to follow North Washington Street and the new highway northwest to the latter road's interchange with NY 441.[[10]](#cite_note-1968map-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/27/NY_253_eastern_terminus.jpg/250px-NY_253_eastern_terminus.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_253_eastern_terminus.jpg)

The current eastern terminus of NY 253 at NY 65 in Pittsford. This was once the southern terminus of NY 253's overlap with NY 65.

In the latter half of the 1980s, NY 253 was truncated southward to the southern terminus of its overlap with NY 65 in Pittsford. The former routing of NY 253 north of NY 96 was redesignated as [NY 153](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_153 "New York State Route 153")[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13) while the segment between NY 65 and NY 64 on Calkins and Mendon Center Roads became NY 943C, an unsigned [reference route](/wiki/Reference_route_(New_York) "Reference route (New York)") 1.68 miles (2.70 km) in length.[[1]](#cite_note-2008tvr-1)

### Henrietta realignments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_253&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Henrietta realignments")]

To the southwest in Henrietta, construction began by 1978 on an extension of Erie Station Road in the vicinity of Scottsville–West Henrietta and East River Roads. The new roadway, as proposed, would begin at the junction of Erie Station and East River Roads and parallel the [New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") westward toward the [Genesee River](/wiki/Genesee_River "Genesee River"), then loop back to the southeast to end at Farrell Road.[[14]](#cite_note-14) Ultimately, the proposed loop to Farrell Road was scrapped in favor of a more direct connection with Scottsville–West Henrietta Road. The new highway became part of a rerouted NY 253 on April 1, 1984, when it was added to the state highway system as part of a highway maintenance swap between the state of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") and [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York"). In the swap, ownership and maintenance of the former routing of NY 253 on Scottsville–West Henrietta Road was transferred from the state to the town of Henrietta while the East River Road portion was given to Monroe County[[15]](#cite_note-law-15) and is now part of [County Route 84](/wiki/County_Route_84_(Monroe_County,_New_York) "County Route 84 (Monroe County, New York)") (CR 84).[[16]](#cite_note-16)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f1/NY_253_ref_marker_in_Pittsford.jpg/250px-NY_253_ref_marker_in_Pittsford.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_253_ref_marker_in_Pittsford.jpg)

Reference marker for NY 253 on Mendon Center Road (now NY 943C) in the town of Pittsford

In the late 1990s, the amount of traffic at the intersection between NY 253 (Erie Station Road) and NY 15 (West Henrietta Road) began to increase due to the creation of the Thruway Industrial Park. Due to the presence of historical buildings at the intersection, the junction could not be widened to handle the increased traffic flow.[[8]](#cite_note-gn-8) As a result, plans were developed to construct a bypass of the intersection to the northwest. On July 29, 1999, Governor [George Pataki](/wiki/George_Pataki "George Pataki") announced that the state of New York would contribute $1 million[[17]](#cite_note-tpd-17) (equivalent to $1.93 million in 2025)[[18]](#cite_note-inflation-US-18) toward the construction of the highway, which would be an extension of Thruway Park Drive.[[17]](#cite_note-tpd-17) It opened to traffic in 2000 as a realignment of NY 253.[[8]](#cite_note-gn-8) The roadway was dedicated to the town of Henrietta, and although a proposal was made that would transfer maintenance of the highway to the [New York State Department of Transportation](/wiki/New_York_State_Department_of_Transportation "New York State Department of Transportation"),[[17]](#cite_note-tpd-17) it remains a town highway today.[[19]](#cite_note-19) The 0.96-mile (1.54 km) long former routing of NY 253 on Erie Station Road became NY 943E, an unsigned reference route.[[1]](#cite_note-2008tvr-1)[[20]](#cite_note-20)

## Major intersections

The entire route is in [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York").

| 5.03 | 8.10 | [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15") south | Southern end of NY 15 concurrency |
| 5.98 | 9.62 | [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15") north | Northern end of NY 15 concurrency |
